_data.text: Fully agree with you on this, I think I'll compare to BrandoSando because he has amazing magic systems. Sometimes they are super clear from the beginning because we are learning it with the main characters. Sometimes there's secret ones or unclear ones and they get introduced slowly, little breadcrumbs, little foreshadowing. And everything is so clear and if it's not, it's because something is meant to be a surprise and unclear

Rebecca, we need to talk. I understand, that you had an image in your head about what you want these characters to be able to do with magic and then you had to scramble for laws for the magic that explain how the magic works. This is the wrong way around. You should have thought about the foundation of how the magic works, which gives the limitations for what they can and cannot do with their magic. That's why nothing in this book makes sense and that's why for the life of me I don't understand the magic.

kathi
_data.text: Okay, so since in parallel I always read Harry Potter in french whenever I have the brain capacity for it, I noticed a couple of things that really show that Rebecca sucks as a writer and that JK Rowling for example did correct from the start which is pretty impressive. So first of all, JK Rowling introduces concepts and characters WAY before they become relevant. For example, she introduces Sirius Black already in the first book because he gives Hagrid the motor bike before he becomes relevant in the third book. She casually introduces that Harry can talk to snakes in the first book as well and it only becomes relevant in the next book. Same with the same with the Whipping Willow (is that was it's called in English haha?) in which they crash in the second book, but it only becomes relevant in the third. Rebecca only introduces things that are relevant either in the scene that makes it relevant or maybe a couple of chaptera before MAX. also, all she writes is like movie scenes where something happens. She doesn't give us any dialogue from violett apart from reactions to things that happen in the moment, which makes her character flat as hell. In Harry Potter, we regularly get bits where Harry is just lying in his bed staring at the ceiling thinking about stuff, or in class staring out the window thinking about stuff etc and that gives us also a feeling about his ups and downs. We absolutely now whether chapters in his life he's depressed or happy etc. violet is just always the same flavor of annoying. also JK Rowling is great at setting the mood for a scene and making us feel like the characters and gives Hogwarts as a place a personality. I know exactly how the mood is in different rooms/locations in Hogwarts throughout the year. I don't know at all what it's like at besgiath except depressing, so I don't want to be there
